mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the prior three months signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS increased over the prior 3 months, or the rank correlation between 3-month PAYEMS change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.50 percentage points over the prior three months signals restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when the trigger is active, or the mean forward return during trigger periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a self-reinforcing demand-expansion loop where income growth fuels consumption; this joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because neither variable alone captures the breadth of the spending cycle.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to exhibit a positive rank correlation with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not add significant explanatory power beyond individual series in a pooled regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5 percent over two months signals that restrictive policy is suppressing real output, which raises recession probabilities and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening-cycle periods, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO declines (mean return > 0 or t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's accuracy is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5 percent over three months signals weakening residential construction and downstream demand for durable goods, which reduces aggregate output expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of trigger instances, or the rank correlation between 3-month HOUST change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.6 percent signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the mean 60-day return during these months is not below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Annualized quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) exceeding 3.5 percent signals demand running above potential, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 3.5% annualized over prior quarter
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are not followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint reading of declining unemployment (UNRATE falls more than 0.2 percentage points over two months) and rising industrial production (INDPRO rises more than 1.0 percent over two months) signals a non-inflationary supply-side expansion that lifts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp over 2 months and INDPRO rises > 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not significant (p > 0.05) in a forward-return regression.
